item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am now scared. 1+

item.title: Enthusiastic upvote
item.content:encoded: This has some strong pastarasta vibes. Love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe this was posted and featured around the time I just began writing and posing my first article. I remember enjoying it the first time and decided to give it another shot. It definitely is a great piece through and through. Farmer's strongman and cold faux-personality is so charming in a terrifying way. He, along with the images, is what really makes this article what it is.

The pictures have so many small details in them it would be hard to hit them all. My favorite is the "Keep Reading" image when you click on the initial picture of Farmer. Such a small thing to miss, I just found it this time around.

► Critique
▼ Critique

I do have two criticisms, one that has been reiterated prior to me and another I didn't see commented on.

The dialogue is a bit uncanny valley-ish. It delves from cartoonish, which I think works in a sense, to a bit unrealistic. I think this is most prevalent in D-6770's exploration log.

I know this is just a dumb game… but how? How am I feeling all these sensations?

This one is one of many. It feels like it shouldn't be the reaction a few minutes after watching the supposedly friendly Farmer torture and erase someone from existence.

My other has to do with the narrative and Farmer. Farmer's abilities, for the most part, are consistent. It's an omniscient, all-powerful being in Goat VR. My issue is that there's no real foreshadowing to Farmer being able to take over the site.

These weren't enough to detract from the uniqueness of this entire article, and I thoroughly enjoyed both times reading.

+1 I'm looking forward to toy box.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 329r0deiwaofi

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get what you mean. Hopefully my next SCP will improve upon that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I really enjoyed most of this skip, I think my only major criticism is that the dialogue feels a bit "forced" and/or "artificial" (I don't really know how to describe it) in some places (mostly in the last log), like:

"Researcher Ivan Cherv: Why exactly do you do this?

SCP-5045-1 begins to cry."

That action from SCP-5045-1 feels so sudden and out of place (though this probably was the intention for this one given the context, I still felt the same way about a bunch of other stuff in the entire skip);

Researcher Ivan Cherv: So you enjoy doing this to people?

SCP-5045-1: Yep! When are you gonna send me another friend, huh pal? Last one was so fun to toy with! Haven't felt like that in a while!

Researcher Ivan Cherv: I'm sorry, but I don't think that'll be necessary.

SCP-5045-1: Oh…

This entire bit just completely lacks emotion to me, I don't know, I can't really explain why, it just seems unnatural (amazing criticism, I know, sorry);

Also (I'm probably being nitpicky on this) to me the last log feels really… fast-paced, I guess? I wasn't able to really feel anything from it at all, shit just kinda went from 0 to a 100 really quickly and when I noticed it was already over, probably because I didn't have any connection with the researcher at all.

I still enjoyed this SCP a lot, and please don't mind my poor english/grammar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i implicated its origin to just… exist. like he just existed one day. bad things just happen for no reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it's nice to see a fourth-wall break that doesn't use the "username" code. i also love the subtle storytelling with the files, especially 852 and 853.

if there's one thing i dislike, though, it's probably the lack of explanation about what the farmer is. not, like, how it works (otherwise it'd be a -EX), but if it's a computer program made by someone, some digital being popping into existence, a goat farmer sent to the digital world somehow…it doesn't bother me enough to be worth a novote, though.

item.title: Re: 300+
item.content:encoded: From one of your first upvoters. Your welcome.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: im glad you had fun reading! my main goal when i write is for the article to be a fun experience to read.

strangely and uncomfortably attracted to Farmer what the hell. Good job though, I really like this SCP, I had to log in to this account for the first time in years just to comment about how much fun this was to read.
Thank you all so much! It is an absolute honor to have +300 for my first ever SCP! Thanks for reading!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for answering my question, I guessed Foundation will have a hard time against the Farmer since it now gain access to their database. It always annoyed me when such sapient and hostile anomaly gain an upper hand, so annoyed that I'll give a upvote!
One of my new favorite Skips. Plus I really want this to be on the top rated of all time page. It deserves it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the site physically occupied by the anomaly? Or it just kidnap everyone into its realm?

I meant for it to be ambiguous, but I like to say the site is physically occupied by the anomaly, and it has become a part of the Farmhouse.

Maybe you can add something in the final like "WARNING This article has been contaminated by infohazrd, site-301 has lost contact and believed been occupied by hostile anomalous entity. Foundation assets are mobilizing to recapture site-301 and contain the anomaly" Yeah I know it may ruin the trick of "fourth wall won't protect you"

Farmer's a little bitch, he would totally delete that from the page. That's definitely the initiative they'd take though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are fair criticisms. Cartoons are a very huge influence on my writing and that has definitely bled in a lot here. I also base a lot of dialogue on how I think in my head. Hopefully the next SCP I make will have less of these problems.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To answer your question, Farmer's influence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Didn't really like this, although probably for reasons said elsewhere.

Why's the header logo on the first offset so dark, though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll shoot straight with you; I have two major criticisms of this piece.

First, I found the Description to be unnecessarily detailed about gamey terms. It had a very "Series 1" feel to the description in a way I found unattractive where you bash me with details that could be shown through exploration and probably should be due to their inherently un-clinical nature.

Second, I found the dialogue throughout the entirety of this piece very unnatural and difficult to swallow. I realize it sounds extremely pedantic to say they are written as characters rather than people, but that's how I feel.

D-6770: Something about that door man, I don't really want to enter it. I think I'll just relax here for a bit, have some alone time and umm… watch paint dry.

Lines like this. It reads like it was written for a sitcom.

This whole piece is laced with stuff like that and it's very off-putting for me. Appreciate the effort but -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the site physically occupied by the anomaly? Or it just kidnap everyone into its realm?
Maybe you can add something in the final like "WARNING This article has been contaminated by infohazrd, site-301 has lost contact and believed been occupied by hostile anomalous entity. Foundation assets are mobilizing to recapture site-301 and contain the anomaly"
Yeah I know it may ruin the trick of "fourth wall won't protect you"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: erm i pinged goatvr.com .org and .net and got positive results from goatvr.com

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: such a fun entry! if i had any criticism, it would maybe be that some of the conversations drag on a little too long. but that's just a nitpick, really. enjoyed it a lot, +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Though as written, Farmer doesn't just want some goats; Farmer wants to hurt and manipulate people and will not be satisfied for very long)

I mean yeah, he enjoys torturing people, but he doesn't want to torture every person in the world. If he ever gets bored, he'll probably just leave and find another world with sentient creatures to torment.

First, though, a minor nitpick: taking a whole foundation site down pretty clearly puts it in Keter class.

Absolutely, but the thing is that

1. The class was written before the events of the interview

2. Farmer has this page and has taken semi-control over it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's partly a fair point — you can have a powerful entity that isn't very ambitious. (Though as written, Farmer doesn't just want some goats; Farmer wants to hurt and manipulate people and will not be satisfied for very long)

First, though, a minor nitpick: taking a whole foundation site down pretty clearly puts it in Keter class.

Then — how are the containment procedures outlined at the beginning at all sufficient? The article completely changes the threat level and nature of the anomaly in the final paragraphs of the final interview block. Based on reading everything up until the end, the SCP could be entirely memetic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Farmer doesn't want the whole world, Farmer just wants a nice collection of goats. It says "Farmer is satisfied" for a reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked it up until the end.

The problem with Farmer winning this way (essentially through brute force) is that it makes the manipulation a moot point.

If Farmer is capable of suddenly taking over a secure foundation facility, through a controlled and limited interface — what's the point of everything leading up to that? Why hide, why use VR, why not just take over the world?

The story would have been better ended as a stalemate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I am not the first one to do a VR skip, Here's one that I really like.

Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this! This has its own unique feel to it in the same way many other classic articles do, and I'm very surprised I'd never seen a VR scip before.

item.title: Re: itchy mind in an itchy body
item.content:encoded: This was a really interesting SCP, and the way it went from crappy VR game to interdimensional cosmic horror was r e a l l y smooth. The ending was also really nice. Very creative. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely loved this! I made some art of the goats the instant I saw them. This got a +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gonna agree here. The art is cool, but the fiction is sorely lacking.

item.title: Re: I found the goat VR website!!!
item.content:encoded: Three supposedly identical links in a row like that looks extremely suspicious.

item.title: Re: I found the goat VR website!!!
item.content:encoded: I don't think these links are malicious, as this seems to be an actual thing, but I wouldn't click anyway just in case.

item.title: Re: I found the goat VR website!!!
item.content:encoded: I assumed this was spam, or phishing, but it's still here. Are these safe?

item.title: Re: I found the goat VR website!!!
item.content:encoded: What on earth

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "billboard" could also be easily misinterpreted as those big advertisements wot be on the sides of roads and stuff. "drawn in two dimensions, appearing as a flat shape" gets the point across

item.title: I found the goat VR website!!!
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user has been permanently banned for spamming.

item.title: Re: image author
item.content:encoded: You're welcome! :)

item.title: Re: image author
item.content:encoded: Very happy to hear you appreciate it! I found the image on a whim while searching through the wikimedia website and I thought it could be put to use. It worked out really well! Thank you!

item.title: image author
item.content:encoded:
Hi, authors of this SCP article.
So basically, the hotel hallway that you used.
I'm the one who photographed it. I'm glad to see that people use it.
I'm just using my phone to capture that image btw.
That picture is a hotel hallway of a hotel(?) near my home.
I don't even know that people would even use it.
And it is even being used in this website.
so cool!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Votes don't help the author, so I've vowed to always leave a comment with mine, at the very least whenever I rate an article negatively. If you feel the same, help out by leaving critique with more of your votes. Comment follows:

This one doesn't do it for me. There's something that irks me about the themes of 'video game is real' and 'computer affects real world', and this article doesn't do anything that alleviates those concerns so doesn't suspend my disbelief at any point.

Farmer wins. I'm not sure that the Foundation should allow that to happen - this could have become a battle where one party tries its best to outwit the other. The closest we come to that is by the researcher not using a headset, which was a nice touch, but it wasn't enough.

I know there's a large audience for this sort of thing, but sadly it doesn't include me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ive been following this since it was a foal…..
glad its up, surprised i forgot to comment earlier? i thought i did. i think i dreamed about the scp wiki +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
These goats are drawn in two dimensions, appearing as a flat shape.

What does this sentence mean? I mean every thing you see on a screen is in two dimensions. Maybe you meant a billboard?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this a lot, especially the line "it has to be obvious at this point, right?" during the goat profiles section. This isn't the most unique concept, to be sure, but it has a lot of personality in the execution, and it was a very entertaining read. Also, I knew not to trust the click to enlarge on the last image, damn it

item.title: Good SCP
item.content:encoded: The story seemed just a tad unbelievable, but it was super creepy and entertaining nonetheless. You did a damn good job. Well done. :-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will answer each question you have about the goats!

goat moment
– hide block

Are they sentient in any way?

Absolutely. Although, being a virtual goat is a very surreal and bizarre feeling. It's as though your entire body was made out of sandpaper and glass. It's hard to describe, lol.

Do they retain any past memories of their human self?

Depends. Some are struggling to remember much about themselves, others remember, and the Helpers are completely void of anything resembling a memory, more like a puppet husk if anything.

Do they do anything besides walking around.

They do whatever Farmer tells them to do, least they be punished in whatever way Farmer feels is suitable. Farmer's incredibly powerful in his little game world, so like, anything goes really.

Thank you for reading!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed reading it! The concept was fun, and the art was very unique and intriguing. I especially liked the goat files.
I also liked how the setting isn’t anything cool, for example a futuristic city or ruined mansion, but a simple ordinary farm. It really enhanced the creepiness.
Would like a bit more of the goats though. Are they sentient in any way? Do they retain any past memories of their human self? Do they do anything besides walking around?
Because there isn’t much info about the goats themselves, the creepiness of getting turned into one is slightly lessened.
The creepiness of the farmer makes up for that though:)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I thought I wasn't going to like it. I thought it was going to be a generic video game creepypasta story (which it essentially was but I gave it the benefit of the doubt) but it was much better than expected. I love your use of images as a bait and switch thing by compelling the reader to enlarge the photos. The way the scenes are setup makes this article feel like a horror movie, which is a big plus.

Overall, I think this deserves the rating it's got. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed!

Well dang, partner!

That was an incredibly fun read. The simple setup gave way to such a fun and creepy story that really feels like classic SCP while new at the same time! I was already sold before you included the feature with enlarging the photos! Definitely a +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I gotta say this SCP is pretty good. I'm new in the community and I haven't been reading very often, but I enjoyed reading the logs. The artwork is simple but it reaches the creepy vibe, good work!

Either way, while reading the Long Term Expedition Log I noticed that the fifth images has the comment

"SCP-5045-1 spotting D-1711 and SCP-5045-2."

But D-1711 was the subject for the Short Term Expedition, it wasn't supposed to be D-6770 the Long Term Expedition subject?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For a lot of it, I thought it was dumb, but I liked it in the end. The art is what did it, I think.
Definitely creeped me out though
I'm so damn happy, thanks everyone for this!!!!!! I'm so shocked this has gotten to 200+!! I'm so glad people enjoy this bizarre story!!

item.title: mixed
item.content:encoded:
I really really really love the art; it's super surreal and expressive and gives a great unique flavor to this. Also like how you hide the ending in the last image. Made a vague and unsettling ending even creepier, great job.

…but, this article just really isn't for me. "Video game but weird and scary and maybe dangerous" is one of those things that tastes pretty worn out to me and there isn't really anything here that hooks me well enough to overcome that. Similar feelings about your cited inspirations, so it's absolutely a me thing; could see this working for people into that trope (genre?). It really does a good job emulating classic video game creepypasta

Last thing: I praised the presentation of the ending, but not a fan of the content itself. I just feel like there wasn't enough buildup to this breaking the limitations you set up so hard and it comes off as shock for shock's sake. It's not nearly as laughable as "and then there was a bloody sonic plushie on my bed!!!1!" but it did give me just a sprinkle of that same feeling. It's not terrible (again, the format saves it from me hating it), just feels unearned and a bit cheesy once the initial "ah fuck" reflex wears off. Again, this is probably my distaste for video game creepypasta still speaking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Although I think a lot of the dialogue could use some tightening up, the specific Petscops/FNAF2/Baldi art and vibes make me thoroughly enjoy this enough to upvote.

item.title: Reality
item.content:encoded: I kinda wanna make this for real…

item.title: Title CSS change
item.content:encoded:
I changed up the CSS on my title a bit! I thought it would fit the theme of this SCP a lot more. I want to thank Sekai_s for letting me use the title CSS of their SCP-CN-1109. I am very grateful!

[UPDATE: I removed it for reasons that I am bad at explaining.]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! It means a lot to me that I was able to capture that feeling for someone!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think I've ever dreamed about the Foundation, but I have a lot of dreams where I'm exploring someplace, and there's this vague, surreal sense of ominousness and the feeling that I'm completely powerless to stop it. This article does as good a job of capturing that feeling as I've seen. The art is great, and the ending was very creative, although I missed the final click-through until I read the comments. Here's yer hearty +1, friend!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ivan's name has been fixed.
